# SlopGuard text fixture (.txt) — materialized to .go at test/runtime
lang: go
file: sample.go
---
package sample

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"regexp"
	"strings"
)

// Bad demonstrates a few common performance "slop" patterns.
func Bad(rows []string) ([]string, map[string]int) {
	var out []string
	counts := make(map[string]int)

	acc := ""
	for i, r := range rows {
		// SLOP001: regex compiled on every iteration.
		re := regexp.MustCompile(`^\d+`)
		if re.MatchString(r) {
			out = append(out, r)
		}

		// SLOP002: string concat inside loop is O(n^2).
		acc = acc + r

		// SLOP004: map re-allocated inside loop.
		local := make(map[string]int)
		local[r] = i
		for k, v := range local {
			counts[k] = v
		}

		// Bonus slop: JSON marshal in hot path.
		_, _ = json.Marshal(local)
	}

	// SLOP003: append inside loop without pre-sizing is fine here, but
	// the pattern is also flagged by SLOP003 in the loop body above.
	_ = strings.ToUpper(acc)
	return out, counts
}

// Good hoists the regex out of the loop.
func Good(rows []string) []string {
	re := regexp.MustCompile(`^\d+`)
	out := make([]string, 0, len(rows))
	for _, r := range rows {
		if re.MatchString(r) {
			out = append(out, r)
		}
	}
	return out
}
